ui.name: Name
ui.noUnitsFound: No units found matching your criteria.
ui.disable3DDesc: Save data on mobile/tablet networks by loading 2D images instead of 3D holographic models.
ui.noUnitsFoundSearch: No units found for this search.

races.terranDesc: The masters of tactical adaptation. Their stimpack-fueled infantry and mobile mechanical divisions dominate industrial and defensive warfare, backed by structures capable of lifting off and relocating.
races.zergDesc: The biological vortex of the Swarm. They spread across organic creep and adapt through larvae and instant mutations, overwhelming the enemy with rapid swarms and bio-synthetic monstrosities.
races.protossDesc: The pinnacle of ancient psionic technology. Their elite warriors harness rechargeable plasma shields and dimensional warp gates, summoning devastating power from the Nexus with pinpoint precision.
races.protossIntro: Protoss units rely on shields, precision, mobility and high-tech power spikes. Their army rewards positioning, timing and efficient spell usage.
races.terranIntro: Terran units combine mobility, range and tactical flexibility. Their army thrives on positioning, production cycles and multi-pronged pressure.
races.zergIntro: Zerg units are built around speed, adaptation and overwhelming numbers. Their strength comes from map control, larvae cycles and fast tech transitions.

landing.heroSubtitle: The most advanced interactive atlas for StarCraft II units. High-fidelity data, tactical insights, and immersive 3D visualization.
landing.heroExplanation: Select a race, explore its tactical units, compare costs and understand each unit's battlefield role.
landing.commandCenterSubtitle: Choose your faction to access its tactical unit database.
landing.precisionDataDesc: Access exact damage, armor, and cost stats for every unit, updated for the current StarCraft II competitive meta.
landing.tacticalAbilitiesDesc: Discover the hidden potential of every ability. From Stalker Blink to Raven's Matrix, master every micro-maneuver.
landing.advancedViewDesc: Explore 3D models and technical diagrams designed for analysts and competitive players seeking the ultimate edge.

footer.tagline: A premium tactical exploration tool for StarCraft II units.
footer.madeBy: Made with ❤️ by <datatech>DataTech H&P</datatech>. All rights reserved.
footer.legalNotice: SC2 Atlas is a fan-made project and is not affiliated with or endorsed by Blizzard Entertainment. StarCraft II and related assets are trademarks or registered trademarks of Blizzard Entertainment.

stalker.name: Stalker
stalker.shortDescription: Mobile combat unit with ranged attack and high repositioning capability.
stalker.longDescription: The Stalker is a mechanical combat unit for the protoss, inspired by the ancient Dragoons but piloted by the psionic essence of the Dark Templar. Its Blink ability makes it one of the most versatile units in the game.
stalker.tacticalRole: Flexible ranged unit used for map control, pressure and anti-air support.
strengths: High mobility with Blink
Useful against air and ground targets
Strong for poking and repositioning
weaknesses: Lower raw DPS compared to some dedicated damage units
Can be punished if Blink is unavailable
Requires good positioning
stalker.usageTip: Use Stalkers to poke, reposition and punish exposed units. Avoid taking direct fights without Blink or support.

blink.name: Blink
blink.description: Instantly teleports the Stalker to a nearby location after researching the upgrade at the Cybernetics Core.

marine.name: Marine
marine.shortDescription: Versatile light infantry unit, the backbone of any Terran army.
marine.longDescription: The Marine is the basic Terran infantry unit. Equipped with powered armor and C-14 Gauss rifles, they are capable of engaging both ground and air targets, especially with Medivac support.
marine.tacticalRole: High DPS core infantry unit that scales extremely well with upgrades and support.
strengths: Excellent damage-to-cost ratio
Fast attack speed, especially with Stimpack
Versatile; hits both ground and air targets
weaknesses: Very fragile, highly vulnerable to splash damage
Requires active micro-management and Medivac support
Relies heavily on Combat Shields and Stimpack upgrades
marine.usageTip: Always research Stimpack and Combat Shields early. Keep Marines paired with Medivacs and split them to minimize splash damage.

combat-shield.name: Combat Shield
combat-shield.description: Permanently increases the Marine's maximum health by +10. Research in Barracks TechLab

stimpack.name: Stimpack
stimpack.description: Temporarily increases attack and movement speed in exchange for a small amount of health. Research in Barracks TechLab

scv.name: SCV
scv.shortDescription: Basic Terran construction and resource gathering unit.
scv.longDescription: The Space Construction Vehicle (SCV) is the essential workhorse for the Terran. In addition to gathering resources, it can repair mechanical units and structures in the field.

repair.name: Repair
repair.description: Repairs friendly mechanical units and structures for a small resource cost.

scv.tacticalRole: Essential worker unit that gathers resources and repairs mechanical units and structures.
strengths: Can repair mechanical units for free (costs minerals in practice)
Available from the start with zero tech requirement
Can bunker rush or proxy build to disrupt opponents early
weaknesses: Extremely fragile and has no real combat ability
Consumed when building structures
Vulnerable if caught outside the base
scv.usageTip: Protect your SCVs at all costs. Use them to repair siege tanks in the field and bunkers during defensive stands.

marauder.name: Marauder
marauder.shortDescription: Heavy infantry armed with grenade launchers, ideal against armored units.
marauder.longDescription: The Marauder is a robust infantry unit capable of withstanding significant punishment. Its Punisher grenades slow down enemy units, making it the perfect companion for the Marine.

concussive-shells.name: Concussive Shells
concussive-shells.description: Slows down the movement of impacted enemy units.

stimpack.name: Stimpack
stimpack.description: Temporarily increases attack and movement speed in exchange for a small amount of health.

marauder.tacticalRole: Heavy armored infantry that slows targets and deals bonus damage to armored units.
strengths: Concussive Shells slows all non-massive units on hit
High health pool, far tankier than Marines
Pairs extremely well with Marines in bio compositions
weaknesses: Cannot attack air units
Slow without Stimpack
Weak against Banelings and splash AoE
marauder.usageTip: Use Marauders as the frontline shield for Marines. Stim them in engagements and pull back damaged ones with Medivac support.

reaper.name: Reaper
reaper.shortDescription: Fast scout unit capable of jumping over cliffs.
reaper.longDescription: The Reaper excels at early harassment and scouting. Equipped with jetpacks, it can enter and exit enemy bases quickly.

kd8-charge.name: KD8 Charge
kd8-charge.description: Throws an explosive charge that deals damage and knocks back enemy units.

jet-pack.name: Jetpack Thrusters
jet-pack.description: Passive thrusters allow the Reaper to scale up and down cliffs freely.

comback-drgs.name: Combat Drugs
comback-drgs.description: Passive drug nanobots regenerate Reaper's health rapidly when out of combat for 5 seconds.

reaper.tacticalRole: Fast harassment and scouting unit that excels at early map control and cliff traversal.
strengths: Can jump cliffs and escape most early threats
Excellent for scouting opponent's base in the first few minutes
KD8 Charges create space and deal solid early damage
weaknesses: Falls off significantly in mid and late game
Low health; dies quickly to sustained fire
Requires constant micro-management to be effective
reaper.usageTip: Open with a Reaper to scout and deal early damage. Retreat over cliffs when threatened, and keep them alive to continue scouting mid-game.

ghost.name: Ghost
ghost.shortDescription: Elite infantry with stealth capabilities and tactical nuclear strikes.
ghost.longDescription: The Ghost is the ultimate tactical unit for the Terran. Capable of becoming invisible and launching tactical nuclear missiles, it can devastate entire armies if undetected.

steady-targeting.name: Steady Targeting
steady-targeting.description: Deals massive damage to a biological unit after a brief channeling period.

emp-round.name: EMP Round
emp-round.description: Drains energy and shields in an area of effect.

nuclear-sil.name: Tactical Nuke Silo
nuclear-sil.description: Allows the Ghost Academy to arm tactical nuclear missiles which the Ghost can call down.

cloak.name: Personal Cloaking
cloak.description: Allows the Ghost to research personal cloaking at the Ghost Academy to become invisible in exchange for continuous energy drain.

ghost.tacticalRole: Elite stealth unit that neutralizes enemy spellcasters, spells, and shields with EMP and Snipe.
strengths: EMP drains Protoss shields and energy in a large radius
Cloak allows repositioning without taking fire
Tactical Nuke forces opponent movement and denies areas
weaknesses: Expensive and requires significant tech investment
EMP requires good aim under pressure
Needs constant energy management
ghost.usageTip: Pre-position Ghosts and use EMP on the Protoss army before engaging. Against Zerg, use Snipe on Infestors before they can cast.

hellion.name: Hellion
hellion.shortDescription: Fast vehicle with a long-range flamethrower ideal against light infantry.
hellion.longDescription: The Hellion is the fastest crowd-control unit for the Terran. Its flame attack pierces multiple enemies in a straight line.

transform-hellbat.name: Transform to Hellbat
transform-hellbat.description: Transforms the vehicle into a heavy short-range infantry unit.

infernal-preigniter.name: Infernal Pre-igniter
infernal-preigniter.description: Significantly increases the flamethrower's damage against light units.

smart-servos.name: Smart Servos
smart-servos.description: Significantly speeds up the transformation process between Hellion and Hellbat forms.

hellion.tacticalRole: Fast vehicle with a linear flamethrower, devastating against workers and light ground units.
strengths: Fastest non-flying Terran unit
Flame attack hits multiple units in a line
Extremely effective against light biological units and workers
weaknesses: Almost useless against armored units
Fragile — dies quickly to focused fire
Cannot attack air targets
hellion.usageTip: Use Hellions to raid enemy worker lines or patrol the map for stray units. Transform to Hellbats when the opponent clusters armor.

hellbat.name: Hellbat
hellbat.shortDescription: Heavy infantry version of the Hellion, more resistant with a conical attack.
hellbat.longDescription: The Hellbat combines flamethrower fire power with the resistance of a front-line unit. It is excellent for protecting fragile units.

infernal-preigniter.name: Infernal Pre-igniter
infernal-preigniter.description: Modifies the flamethrower payload to deal significantly increased damage against light units.

smart-servos.name: Smart Servos
smart-servos.description: Significantly speeds up the transformation process between Hellion and Hellbat forms.

hellbat.tacticalRole: Beefy frontline brawler with a cone flamethrower, effective in tight bio-mech compositions.
strengths: More durable than Hellion form
AoE cone attack hits multiple clustered units
Healed by Medivacs — synergizes with bio-mech drops
weaknesses: Very slow movement speed
Weak against armored units and air
Needs to be transformed from Hellion, limiting early flexibility
hellbat.usageTip: Drop Hellbats into enemy worker lines with Medivacs for an efficient trade. In bio-mech, position them at the front to absorb fire.

siege-tank.name: Siege Tank
siege-tank.shortDescription: The pillar of Terran defense, capable of deploying for long-range attacks.
siege-tank.longDescription: The Siege Tank can operate in two modes: Tank Mode for mobility and Siege Mode for devastating long-range area damage.

siege-mode.name: Siege Mode
siege-mode.description: Anchors the tank to drastically increase its range and damage, but loses the ability to move.

siege-tank.tacticalRole: Long-range siege weapon that anchors Terran defensive lines and obliterates ground armies at a distance.
strengths: Devastating area damage in Siege Mode with immense range
Shreds armored units and structures efficiently
Forces opponent to engage from disadvantageous angles
weaknesses: Cannot move or attack nearby units in Siege Mode
Vulnerable to air units in all modes
Slow to set up and reposition
siege-tank.usageTip: Always siege Tanks behind marine walls or bunkers. Never leave them exposed. Viking support counters enemy air harassment.

cyclone.name: Cyclone
cyclone.shortDescription: Fast attack vehicle capable of firing while moving.
cyclone.longDescription: The Cyclone uses a lock-on system that allows it to fire missiles at an enemy unit while maintaining distance.

lock-on.name: Lock-On
lock-on.description: Locks onto an enemy target to fire automatically while moving within range.

magfield-accelerator.name: Magfield Accelerator
magfield-accelerator.description: Increases Lock-On damage output against armored units.

cyclone.tacticalRole: Mobile anti-air and anti-ground skirmisher with a Lock-On ability that fires while kiting.
strengths: Can fire while moving after locking on
Strong anti-air for early game protection
Very mobile — difficult to catch or pin down
weaknesses: Mediocre DPS without upgrades and micromanagement
Lock-On can be broken by moving target out of range
High gas cost limits early production
cyclone.usageTip: Use Cyclones as mobile anti-air in the early game and as fast kiting units. Lock onto high-priority air targets immediately.

widow-mine.name: Widow Mine
widow-mine.shortDescription: Mechanical ambush unit that fires missiles from a burrowed position.
widow-mine.longDescription: The Widow Mine burrows into the ground and fires a Sentinel missile when an enemy unit approaches, dealing massive area damage.

burrow.name: Burrow
burrow.description: Burrows the mine so it can charge its missiles and attack.

sentinel-missiles.name: Sentinel Missiles
sentinel-missiles.description: Passive homing missile attack that fires when burrowed, dealing massive single-target and splash damage.

drilling-claws.name: Drilling Claws
drilling-claws.description: Drastically reduces the time required to burrow and unburrow.

concealment.name: Concealment
concealment.description: Ensures the Widow Mine remains completely invisible even after firing its Sentinel Missile.

widow-mine.tacticalRole: Stealthy ambush mine that punishes enemy movement with devastating splash damage.
strengths: Invisible when burrowed and between attacks
Sentinel Missile deals enormous splash that one-shots groups
Deters enemy from advancing without detection
weaknesses: Very predictable placement over time
Has a reload cooldown between shots
Countered by detectors and slow advance
widow-mine.usageTip: Place Widow Mines at watch points and ramps. Always pair with Vikings or Turrets for mutual anti-air protection of the mines themselves.

thor.name: Thor
thor.shortDescription: Massive combat robot with overwhelming fire power on both ground and air.
thor.longDescription: The Thor is the largest ground mechanical unit for the Terran. Its Thor's Hammers crush ground units, while its missile launchers clear the skies.

high-impact-payload.name: High Impact Payload
high-impact-payload.description: Switches anti-air weaponry for long-range cannons effective against massive units.

smart-servos.name: Smart Servos
smart-servos.description: Significantly speeds up the transformation process between high-impact and explosive payloads.

thor.tacticalRole: Massive heavy mech with overwhelming firepower against both ground armies and capital ships.
strengths: Enormous health pool and high base armor
High-impact payload slaughters massive air units
Strong area suppression against clustered ground units
weaknesses: Very slow movement speed
Expensive to produce and maintain
Vulnerable to EMP, Abduct, and kiting
thor.usageTip: Deploy Thors as stationary centerpieces of a ground army. Use High-Impact Payload to hard-counter incoming Brood Lords or Carriers.

viking.name: Viking
viking.shortDescription: Transformable fighter capable of dominating airspace or landing for ground support.
viking.longDescription: The Viking is the versatile solution for air control. It can transform between Assault Mode (ground) and Fighter Mode (air) as tactical needs dictate.

assault-mode.name: Assault Mode
assault-mode.description: Lands the Viking to attack ground targets.

fighter-mode.name: Fighter Mode
fighter-mode.description: Takes off to attack air targets with long-range missiles.

smart-servos.name: Smart Servos
smart-servos.description: Significantly speeds up the transformation process between Assault and Fighter modes.

viking.tacticalRole: Transformable fighter that rules the skies and can land to support ground forces at key moments.
strengths: Top-tier anti-air vs massive units (Brood Lords, Carriers)
Can land to push towers or defend drops
Cost-efficient answer to Protoss air superiority
weaknesses: Mediocre DPS in ground mode
Squishy for its cost against massed air
Can be caught transitioning between modes
viking.usageTip: Always have a Viking force to counter enemy capital ships. Land Vikings to snipe isolated Siege Tanks or Lurkers.

medivac.name: Medivac
medivac.shortDescription: Air transport capable of healing biological units and providing mobility.
medivac.longDescription: The Medivac is crucial for the survival of Terran infantry. It heals nearby soldiers and can transport troops for lightning strikes.

heal.name: Heal
heal.description: Automatically heals nearby biological units in exchange for energy.

ignite-afterburners.name: Ignite Afterburners
ignite-afterburners.description: Temporarily increases the Medivac's movement speed.

load.name: Load
load.description: Loads friendly units into the cargo hold.

unload-all.name: Unload All
unload-all.description: Drops all loaded units onto the target ground location.

caduceus-reactor.name: Caduceus Reactor
caduceus-reactor.description: Increases the starting energy of Medivacs by 25.

infernal-preigniter.name: Infernal Pre-igniter
infernal-preigniter.description: Equips Medivacs with specialized thermal engines (Legacy tech).

medivac.tacticalRole: Critical support dropship that keeps bio armies alive with healing and repositions them via drops.
strengths: Automatically heals all nearby biological units
Afterburners enable rapid retreat or aggressive drops
Doubles as both healer and transport in one unit
weaknesses: No offensive capability whatsoever
Extremely fragile — dies quickly to focused anti-air
Requires constant protection to be effective
goodAgainst: Supports bio vs. any enemy composition
medivac.usageTip: Keep Medivacs stacked behind your marines. Never let them lead an attack. Drop bio armies into mineral lines when the opponent engages elsewhere.

liberator.name: Liberator
liberator.shortDescription: Support ship with powerful anti-air area attack and a ground siege mode.
liberator.longDescription: The Liberator is excellent for zone control. In Defender Mode, it creates a zone on the ground where it deals massive damage to any enemy that enters.

defender-mode.name: Defender Mode
defender-mode.description: Anchors the ship over a circular zone to attack ground units with high power.

fighter-mode.name: Fighter Mode
fighter-mode.description: Returns the Liberator to its mobile aerial configuration, armed with anti-air splash missiles.

advanced-ballistics.name: Advanced Ballistics
advanced-ballistics.description: Significantly increases the targeting range of Defender Mode.

liberator.tacticalRole: Zone-denial support flyer with a powerful ground siege mode and anti-air capabilities.
strengths: Creates a lethal zone on the ground that melts units entering it
Strong anti-air splash in Fighter Mode
Forces opponent to divert resources to handle it
weaknesses: Stationary and unable to reposition quickly in Defender Mode
Targeted zone is predictable
Expensive gas sink compared to alternatives
liberator.usageTip: Use Liberators to zone enemy expansion mineral lines. Position them over ramps in Defender Mode to deny ground army movement.

raven.name: Raven
raven.shortDescription: Robotic support ship equipped with advanced sensors and combat drones.
raven.longDescription: The Raven is the primary detector for the Terran and a force multiplier. Its support abilities can disable enemies or provide temporary defense.

interference-matrix.name: Interference Matrix
interference-matrix.description: Temporarily disables attack and abilities for a mechanical or psionic unit.

anti-armor-missile.name: Anti-Armor Missile
anti-armor-missile.description: Launches a missile that reduces armor for all impacted units.

auto-turret.name: Auto-Turret
auto-turret.description: Deploys a temporary automatic turret on the battlefield.

raven.tacticalRole: Primary Terran detector and spellcaster, enabling powerful battlefield disruption.
strengths: Interference Matrix disables mechanical units for a long duration
Anti-Armor Missile reduces armor of all hit units simultaneously
Provides crucial detection of cloaked and burrowed units
weaknesses: Very high gas cost limits production
No direct damage without abilities
Fragile against concentrated anti-air fire
raven.usageTip: Use Interference Matrix on the most dangerous enemy mechanical unit before an engagement. Anti-Armor Missile works best against stacked armies.

banshee.name: Banshee
banshee.shortDescription: Ground attack aircraft with cloaking capability.
banshee.longDescription: The Banshee is the ultimate air harasser. Its ability to become invisible allows it to destroy enemy supply lines without being detected.

cloak.name: Cloak
cloak.description: Makes the Banshee invisible to enemies without detectors.

banshee.tacticalRole: Ground attack aircraft that specializes in cloaked harassment of workers and undefended units.
strengths: Cloaking makes it nearly invisible without detection
Extremely effective against undefended expansions
High DPS against ground targets for an air unit
weaknesses: Cannot attack air units at all
Slow movement makes it vulnerable if caught
Countered hard by any detection unit
banshee.usageTip: Send cloaked Banshees to enemy expansions while they are distracted. Cloak them before entry and retreat before detection arrives.

battlecruiser.name: Battlecruiser
battlecruiser.shortDescription: The most imposing capital ship in the Terran arsenal.
battlecruiser.longDescription: The Battlecruiser is capable of engaging multiple targets simultaneously and can jump to any part of the map instantly.

tactical-jump.name: Tactical Jump
tactical-jump.description: Teleports the ship to any map location after a brief delay.

yamato-cannon.name: Yamato Cannon
yamato-cannon.description: Charges and fires a concentrated energy blast that deals 240 damage to a single target.

battlecruiser.tacticalRole: Unstoppable capital ship with incredible durability and Tactical Jump for instant repositioning.
strengths: Massive health pool and armor — takes extremely long to kill
Yamato Cannon one-shots most units and structures
Tactical Jump allows instant retreat or surprise attacks
weaknesses: Extremely expensive and slow to build
Countered efficiently by Corruptors with upgrades
Not viable until very late game
battlecruiser.usageTip: Tactical Jump to a vulnerable part of the opponent's base when their army is occupied. Yamato key defensive structures.

zergling.name: Zergling
zergling.shortDescription: Fast and numerous biological combat unit, ideal for surrounding and overwhelming the enemy.
zergling.longDescription: The Zergling is the smallest but most numerous unit of the Swarm. Its speed and ability to mutate wings make them masters of harassment and flanking.
zergling.tacticalRole: Fast melee unit used for scouting, surrounds, countering ranged units, and harassment.
strengths: Unmatched speed on creep, especially with Metabolic Boost
Extremely cheap and cost-effective in mass numbers
Excellent for map control and counter-attacks
weaknesses: Very low individual health and armor
Highly vulnerable to area-of-effect (splash) damage
Struggles against chokepoints and defensive structures
zergling.usageTip: Use Zerglings to scout the map, surround enemy forces, and run into their bases when their army is out of position.

burrow.name: Burrow
burrow.description: Allows the unit to burrow to hide from the enemy and regenerate health.

morph-baneling.name: Morph Baneling
morph-baneling.description: Morphs a Zergling into an explosive Baneling for a small resource cost.

drone.name: Drone
drone.shortDescription: Basic Swarm gathering unit that can mutate into structures.
drone.longDescription: The Drone is the basis of Zerg economy. Unlike other races, the Drone is consumed to become the biological structures of the Swarm.

basic-mutation.name: Basic Mutation
basic-mutation.description: Morphs the Drone into basic Zerg structures (Hatchery, Spawning Pool, Extractor, etc.).

advanced-mutation.name: Advanced Mutation
advanced-mutation.description: Morphs the Drone into advanced Zerg structures (Spire, Hydralisk Den, Infestation Pit, etc.).

burrow.name: Burrow
burrow.description: Allows Drones to burrow underground for safety.

drone.tacticalRole: Zerg worker that sacrifices itself to build structures — the backbone of the Swarm economy.
strengths: Unique mechanic: consumed to build structures instantly
Can be mass-produced from multiple Hatcheries simultaneously
Excellent early mineral saturation speed
weaknesses: Consumed permanently when morphing into structures
Extremely fragile in combat
Requires constant production discipline to keep balanced
drone.usageTip: Always keep drone count at maximum while your larva and minerals allow. Never lose drones to avoidable harassment.

overlord.name: Overlord
overlord.shortDescription: Flying supply unit that provides control over the Swarm.
overlord.longDescription: Overlords provide the psionic control needed to maintain a large army. They can evolve to transport units or generate creep.

morph-overseer.name: Morph to Overseer
morph-overseer.description: Morphs the Overlord into the vigilant Overseer detector unit.

morph-ventral-sacs.name: Morph Ventral Sacs
morph-ventral-sacs.description: Equips the Overlord with ventral sacs, allowing it to transport friendly ground units.

generate-creep.name: Generate Creep
generate-creep.description: Spews creep onto the ground below, allowing Zerg structures to be built and granting speed bonuses.

overlord.tacticalRole: Flying supply provider and essential backbone for scouting and Zerg army logistics.
strengths: Provides supply without building structures
Doubles as a scouting unit in early game
Can upgrade into an Overseer for detection
weaknesses: Very slow without Pneumatized Carapace
No attack or defense whatsoever
Easy target for enemy anti-air units
overlord.usageTip: Spread Overlords around the map early for vision. Keep them in safe spots later. Morph one to Overseer when detection is needed.

overseer.name: Overseer
overseer.shortDescription: Advanced evolution of the Overlord with detection and sabotage capabilities.
overseer.longDescription: The Overseer is the primary detector for the Zerg. It can spawn Changelings for espionage and contaminate enemy structures to halt production.

spawn-changeling.name: Spawn Changeling
spawn-changeling.description: Creates an espionage unit that mimics the appearance of the first enemy unit it sees.

contaminate.name: Contaminate
contaminate.description: Disables an enemy structure, halting its production or research temporarily.

oversight.name: Oversight
oversight.description: Roots the Overseer in place to increase its vision and detection range by 25%.

overseer.tacticalRole: Detection unit and Changeling espionage tool — essential for countering cloaked strategies.
strengths: Provides detection vs. cloaked and burrowed units
Changeling can scout enemy base indefinitely if undetected
Contaminate halts enemy production during critical timings
weaknesses: Costs larva and resources that could go to army
Fragile — easily killed by anti-air
Limited combat utility beyond detection
overseer.usageTip: Keep one Overseer with your main army and position a second near key expansions. Use Contaminate on enemy Templar Archives or Tech Labs.

queen.name: Queen
queen.shortDescription: Defense and base management unit crucial for Swarm growth.
queen.longDescription: The Queen is vital for any Zerg hive. It injects larvae to speed up production and spreads Creep to give mobility to the army.

spawn-larvae.name: Spawn Larvae
spawn-larvae.description: Injects larvae into a central structure to increase unit production.

transfuse.name: Transfuse
transfuse.description: Instantly heals a friendly biological unit or structure.

spawn-creep-tumor.name: Spawn Creep Tumor
spawn-creep-tumor.description: Creates a tumor that spreads biomatter (creep) across the terrain.

burrow.name: Burrow
burrow.description: Allows the Queen to burrow underground.

queen.tacticalRole: Macro powerhouse that injects larvae for production and spreads creep for map control.
strengths: Spawn Larvae multiplies unit production exponentially
Extremely tanky for its cost with high health and shields
Can defend against early air harassment and Oracle openings
weaknesses: Slow without creep — cannot keep up with mobile armies
Relies on macro play; low combat contribution in direct fights
Prioritizing Queens over units slows early army build
queen.usageTip: Inject every Hatchery each cycle. Spread creep with creep tumors and position Queens near the edge of creep for map control.

baneling.name: Baneling
baneling.shortDescription: Suicidal unit that explodes on contact, devastating against light infantry.
baneling.longDescription: The Baneling is a Zergling mutation loaded with volatile acid. Its explosion deals immense area damage, especially to structures and light units.

explode.name: Explode
explode.description: Triggers immediate self-destruction to deal massive splash damage to surrounding enemy units.

enabled-structure-attack.name: Structure Attack
enabled-structure-attack.description: Toggles whether the Baneling will target and explode against structures.

centrifugal-hooks.name: Centrifugal Hooks
centrifugal-hooks.description: Increases Baneling movement speed and maximum health.

burrow.name: Burrow
burrow.description: Allows Banelings to burrow underground for stealth landmines.

baneling.tacticalRole: Suicidal explosive unit that single-handedly demolishes grouped biological infantry.
strengths: Incredible AoE damage that obliterates bio clumps
Centrifugal Hooks upgrade enables burrowed Baneling mines
Forces opponent to split units or face total annihilation
weaknesses: Dies instantly upon exploding
Requires proper micro to connect with enemy groups
Useless against armored units (does minimal damage)
baneling.usageTip: Burrow Banelings in choke points and wait for clustered Marines to walk over them. Combine with Zerglings to force splits.

roach.name: Roach
roach.shortDescription: Resistant combat unit with high life regeneration.
roach.longDescription: The Roach is the basic tank for the Swarm. Its ability to regenerate health quickly and move underground makes it very hard to eliminate.

morph-ravager.name: Morph to Ravager
morph-ravager.description: Morphs the Roach into the acid-artillery Ravager.

rapid-regeneration.name: Rapid Regeneration
rapid-regeneration.description: Passive trait that grants extremely high health regeneration while burrowed.

burrow.name: Burrow
burrow.description: Allows Roaches to burrow underground, and move while burrowed after upgrade research.

roach.tacticalRole: Durable armored ground unit with high health regeneration, ideal for tanking and zone control.
strengths: Very high health and armor for its cost
Rapid burrowed regeneration lets it recover quickly
Can move while burrowed with Tunneling Claws upgrade
weaknesses: Slow movement speed without upgrades
Susceptible to Immortal barrier and armored-bonus damage
Falls off in usefulness against splash and high-range units
roach.usageTip: Lead with Roaches to absorb damage in engagements. Burrow damaged Roaches behind the battle line to regenerate and return to fight.

ravager.name: Ravager
ravager.shortDescription: Roach mutation capable of launching corrosive bile from a long distance.
ravager.longDescription: The Ravager adds artillery to the Zerg arsenal. Its Corrosive Bile can destroy protoss Force Fields and damage units in elevated positions.

corrosive-bile.name: Corrosive Bile
corrosive-bile.description: Launches a projectile that deals damage in an area after a brief delay, destroying structures and force fields.

ravager.tacticalRole: Roach evolution with Corrosive Bile artillery that demolishes force fields and structures.
strengths: Corrosive Bile can break Protoss Force Fields before they matter
Excellent against structures and defensive positions
Good range for a relatively cheap unit
weaknesses: Corrosive Bile requires precise aim under pressure
Fragile compared to Roaches with similar cost
Consumed larva and gas reduces Hydralisk/Mutalisk numbers
ravager.usageTip: Use Ravagers to pre-emptively break Force Fields the moment Sentries cast them, allowing your Zerglings to flood through.

hydralisk.name: Hydralisk
hydralisk.shortDescription: Versatile ranged attack unit, effective against air and ground.
hydralisk.longDescription: The Hydralisk is the core DPS (damage per second) unit of the Zerg army. Its spines can pierce the toughest armors from afar.

morph-lurker.name: Morph to Lurker
morph-lurker.description: Morphs the Hydralisk into the underground-siege Lurker.

lunge.name: Muscular Augments / Grooved Spines
lunge.description: Combines upgrades to increase Hydralisk movement speed and attack range.

hydralisk.tacticalRole: Versatile high-DPS attacker effective against both air and ground targets.
strengths: Excellent DPS output for its cost
Attacks both air and ground units simultaneously
Lunge upgrade provides massive bonus speed on creep
weaknesses: Fragile — low health pool relative to damage
Very slow off creep without upgrades
Susceptible to splash damage and AoE
hydralisk.usageTip: Keep Hydralisks on creep whenever possible. Pair them with Roaches to protect against melee flanks and with Vipers vs. Protoss.

lurker.name: Lurker
lurker.shortDescription: Burrowed ambush unit that launches spines in a straight line.
lurker.longDescription: The Lurker attacks from the safety of the underground. Its spines pierce multiple enemies, making it devastating in narrow zones.

burrow.name: Burrow
burrow.description: Allows the Lurker to burrow underground, which is required in order to fire its spikes.

lurker.tacticalRole: Subterranean area siege unit. Shreds incoming ground waves with spikes fired while burrowed.
strengths: Devastating linear area-of-effect splash damage
Fires from absolute safety while burrowed
Outranges standard base defenses with upgrades
weaknesses: Cannot attack while unburrowed or moving
Vulnerable to air units and high-range sieges
lurker.usageTip: Burrow Lurkers at critical choke points or ramp entrances to deny enemy ground armies any access to your bases.

adaptive-talons.name: Adaptive Talons
adaptive-talons.description: Decreases the time required for Lurkers to burrow or unburrow, enabling rapid repositioning.

seismic-spines.name: Seismic Spines
seismic-spines.description: Increases the attack range of the Lurker while burrowed from 8 to 10.

infestor.name: Infestor
infestor.shortDescription: Master of mental and biological control, capable of incapacitating armies.
infestor.longDescription: The Infestor has no direct attack, but its parasites can trap units (Fungal Growth) or even take control of enemy units.

fungal-growth.name: Fungal Growth
fungal-growth.description: Immobilizes and damages enemy units in an area.

neural-parasite.name: Neural Parasite
neural-parasite.description: Takes temporary control of an enemy unit.

microbial-shroud.name: Microbial Shroud
microbial-shroud.description: Creates a cloud that protects friendly ground units from air damage.

infestor.tacticalRole: Biological control caster — roots, disables, and takes over enemy units with devastating spells.
strengths: Fungal Growth roots and deals damage in an area
Neural Parasite temporarily steals any enemy unit
Microbial Shroud protects ground units from air fire
weaknesses: Cannot attack directly without casting spells
Very slow and fragile without protection
Expensive energy management and micro required
infestor.usageTip: Move Infestors with your burrowed Zerg army. Fungal Growth key groups before engagement, then Neural Parasite the most dangerous enemy unit.

swarm-host.name: Swarm Host
swarm-host.shortDescription: Siege unit that generates endless waves of locusts.
swarm-host.longDescription: The Swarm Host launches temporary units called Locusts to attack the enemy from a safe distance.

spawn-locusts.name: Spawn Locusts
spawn-locusts.description: Generates two temporary flying units that attack ground targets.

flying-locusts.name: Flying Locusts
flying-locusts.description: Allows spawned Locusts to fly, enabling them to bypass terrain and scale walls.

burrow.name: Burrow
burrow.description: Allows Swarm Hosts to burrow underground.

swarm-host.tacticalRole: Siege unit that generates endless free flying Locusts to pressure enemies and deny areas.
strengths: Constantly generates free units passively — no additional cost
Forces opponent to react and waste resources
Flying Locusts upgrade bypasses terrain and cliff defenses
weaknesses: Requires heavy protection; no direct attacks
Locusts are individually very weak
Extremely slow to reposition when under threat
swarm-host.usageTip: Burrow Swarm Hosts behind ridges or at max range. Keep Zerglings nearby to protect them from units trying to snipe your Swarm Hosts.

mutalisk.name: Mutalisk
mutalisk.shortDescription: Fast air unit with a bounce attack ideal for harassment.
mutalisk.longDescription: The Mutalisk is the terror of supply lines. Its Glaive Wurm attack bounces between multiple targets, maximizing chaos in compact groups.

tissue-regeneration.name: Tissue Regeneration
tissue-regeneration.description: Passive high regeneration trait that restores 2 health per second when out of combat.

mutalisk.tacticalRole: Rapid air harasser with a multi-bounce attack, terrorizing expansions and isolated units.
strengths: Extreme speed makes them very hard to catch
Glaive Wurm bounces to nearby units for extra efficiency
Rapid health regeneration out of combat
weaknesses: Individual DPS is low
Extremely vulnerable to high-burst or splash anti-air
Heavy micro required to keep them alive
mutalisk.usageTip: Harass multiple enemy expansions simultaneously with Mutalisks. Keep them moving to avoid Marine stim surges and Widow Mine volleys.

corruptor.name: Corruptor
corruptor.shortDescription: Heavy air fighter specialized in taking down capital ships.
corruptor.longDescription: The Corruptor is the Zerg response to large enemy ships. It can use its Caustic Spray to corrode structures from the air.

caustic-spray.name: Caustic Spray
caustic-spray.description: Channels a corrosive attack against an enemy structure that increases damage over time.

morph-broodlord.name: Morph to Brood Lord
morph-broodlord.description: Morphs the Corruptor into the massive aerial siege Brood Lord.

corruptor.tacticalRole: Specialist air-to-air fighter optimized for destroying massive capital ships.
strengths: Bonus damage vs. massive units makes it the primary capital ship counter
Can morph into the devastating Brood Lord
Cost-efficient against slow, massive air targets
weaknesses: Poor DPS against non-massive targets
Fragile when caught against mass non-massive fighters
Slow movement limits response time
corruptor.usageTip: Use Corruptors to deny enemy air-superiority compositions. Always transition them to Brood Lords once you have 6+ Corruptors.

viper.name: Viper
viper.shortDescription: Tactical air support unit for manipulating the battlefield.
viper.longDescription: The Viper can pull enemy units toward the Swarm or blind enemy armies with a dense cloud.

abduct.name: Abduct
abduct.description: Pulls an enemy unit toward the Viper's position.

blinding-cloud.name: Blinding Cloud
blinding-cloud.description: Creates a cloud that reduces the attack range of enemy ground units to 1.

parasitic-bomb.name: Parasitic Bomb
parasitic-bomb.description: Launches a parasite at an enemy air unit that deals constant damage to it and nearby ships.

consume.name: Consume
consume.description: Siphons 200 health points from a friendly Zerg structure to rapidly restore 50 energy.

viper.tacticalRole: Battlefield manipulator that dismantles enemy armies with Abduct, Blinding Cloud, and Parasitic Bomb.
strengths: Abduct pulls siege units out of position instantly
Blinding Cloud makes ground armies useless in a zone
Parasitic Bomb chains anti-air damage to fleets
weaknesses: No combat attack of its own
Fragile — must stay out of direct combat
Consume harms friendly Zerg structures
viper.usageTip: Abduct the lead Siege Tank or Thor before engagement. Drop Blinding Cloud on the bio or mech army and follow up with Ultralisks.

ultralisk.name: Ultralisk
ultralisk.shortDescription: The most imposing ground beast of the Swarm.
ultralisk.longDescription: The Ultralisk is a mountain of muscle and bone capable of piercing any defensive line with its Kaiser blades that deal area damage.

frenzied.name: Frenzied
frenzied.description: Passive immunity to stuns, slows, mind control, and movement-restricting status effects.

anabolic-synthesis.name: Anabolic Synthesis
anabolic-synthesis.description: Increases the movement speed of the Ultralisk.

chitinous-plating.name: Chitinous Plating
chitinous-plating.description: Provides +2 armor points to the Ultralisk.

ultralisk.tacticalRole: Unstoppable melee juggernaut with AoE Kaiser Blades and immunity to crowd control effects.
strengths: Enormous AoE melee attack shreds clustered armies
Immune to stuns, slow, and mind control (Frenzied)
Burrow allows repositioning and regeneration
weaknesses: Cannot attack air units at all
Expensive to mass produce
Kited effectively by high-range or mobile units
ultralisk.usageTip: Pair Ultralisks with Vipers. Blinding Cloud disables kiting, and the Ultralisks plow through blinded armies freely.

brood-lord.name: Brood Lord
brood-lord.shortDescription: Heavy siege ship that bombards with parasitic creatures.
brood-lord.longDescription: The Brood Lord launches Broodlings against the enemy, creating constant chaos and blocking ground movement while attacking from the air.

swarm-seed.name: Swarm Seed
swarm-seed.description: Launches Broodlings continuously with each attack, spawning active ground melee units upon impact.

brood-lord.tacticalRole: Aerial siege titan that bombards ground armies with relentless Broodlings from unmatched range.
strengths: Longest attack range of any Zerg unit
Broodlings block paths and tie up enemy units while the Brood Lord fires
Untouchable by ground armies — must be addressed with air
weaknesses: Extremely slow movement speed
Corruptors required to protect them from enemy flyers
Expensive larva, mineral, and gas investment
brood-lord.usageTip: Siege Brood Lords at maximum range and move them forward as the Broodlings and your ground army clear the space. Always protect with Corruptors.

probe.name: Probe
probe.shortDescription: Robotic construction unit that summons structures via micro-vortices.
probe.longDescription: The Probe is the protoss workhorse. Its advanced technology allows it to initiate structure summoning and withdraw immediately to continue working.
probe.tacticalRole: Protoss worker that gathers resources and warps in structures via dimensional pylon networks.
strengths: Can warp structures anywhere with pylon power
Teleports away after starting a warp-in unlike SCV
Can proxy buildings far from base for surprise rushes
weaknesses: Extremely fragile in combat
Slower production than SCV due to single unit from Nexus
Economy devastated if Probes die early
probe.usageTip: Always keep your Probe count growing through the first 5 minutes. Warp in structures quickly to maintain tech advantage.

zealot.name: Zealot
zealot.shortDescription: Formidable Templar warrior armed with psionic blades.
zealot.longDescription: The Zealot is the heart of the protoss infantry. After researching Charge, they can close distance with the enemy in a heartbeat.

charge.name: Charge
charge.description: Passively increases movement speed and allows charging at nearby enemies.

zealot.tacticalRole: High-damage melee warrior that excels at surrounding and destroying armies with Charge.
strengths: Charge upgrade provides immense closing speed
High damage per swing vs. biological units
Cheap and fast to produce in numbers
weaknesses: Melee range only — cannot attack air
Slow without Charge; easily kited
Easily countered by AoE or splash damage
zealot.usageTip: Warp Zealots in continuously and use Charge to surround enemy bio balls. Lead Zealots around the edges to flank while Colossi deal AoE.

sentry.name: Sentry
sentry.shortDescription: Robotic support unit capable of manipulating terrain with force fields.
sentry.longDescription: The Sentry is fundamental for map control. It can block paths with force fields and protect the army with guardian shields.

force-field.name: Force Field
force-field.description: Creates a temporary barrier that blocks ground movement.

guardian-shield.name: Guardian Shield
guardian-shield.description: Creates an aura that reduces incoming ranged damage for nearby friendly units.

hallucination.name: Hallucination
hallucination.description: Creates a fake copy of a protoss unit to deceive the enemy.

sentry.tacticalRole: Defensive support caster whose Force Fields control terrain and protect armies from superior numbers.
strengths: Force Field blocks ground movement, splitting armies
Guardian Shield reduces all ranged damage to nearby units
Hallucination can scout or waste enemy detection
weaknesses: Energy-dependent — useless with no energy
Force Fields can be destroyed by Ravagers
Weak combat statistics on its own
sentry.usageTip: Place Force Fields at ramp bases to block Zerg run-bys. Use Guardian Shield before engaging bio-mech armies to reduce incoming DPS.

adept.name: Adept
adept.shortDescription: Tactical harassment unit capable of projecting a shadow of itself.
adept.longDescription: The Adept excels in early combat. Its Psionic Transfer ability allows it to project a shadow and teleport to its location, ideal for flanking.

psionic-transfer.name: Psionic Transfer
psionic-transfer.description: Projects a shadow that moves independently; the Adept teleports to the shadow's position after a few seconds.

resonating-glaives.name: Resonating Glaives
resonating-glaives.description: Permanently increases the Adept's attack speed by 45%.

adept.tacticalRole: Highly mobile early harasser that can project a phantom and teleport to flank or escape.
strengths: Psionic Transfer creates opportunities for flanks and fakes
Resonating Glaives makes it a strong early unit
Shaded bonus vs. light is excellent against early bio
weaknesses: Falls off in large-scale engagements later
Fragile — dies fast if caught in bad position
Shade takes 3 seconds before teleport completes
adept.usageTip: Send Adept shades into the opponent's base to scout and damage workers. Cancel the shade if the shade is intercepted.

high-templar.name: High Templar
high-templar.shortDescription: Powerful psionic master capable of unleashing energy storms.
high-templar.longDescription: The High Templar is one of the most feared units. Its Psionic Storm can decimate entire armies in seconds.

feedback.name: Feedback
feedback.description: Drains all energy from an enemy unit and deals damage equal to the energy drained.

archon-warp.name: Archon Warp
archon-warp.description: Two Templars merge to become a powerful Archon.

psi-storm.name: Psionic Storm
psi-storm.description: Calls down a storm of psionic energy that deals 80 damage over 2.85 seconds in an area of effect.

high-templar.tacticalRole: Premier psionic spellcaster. Wreaks havoc with Psi Storms and disables spellcasters with Feedback.
strengths: Psi Storm deals massive damage over time in a wide area
Feedback drains enemy energy and inflicts equivalent damage
Morphs into an Archon when energy is depleted
weaknesses: No standard auto-attacks
Very slow and fragile
high-templar.usageTip: Pre-storm incoming bio before engagement. Use Feedback on enemy Ghosts before they EMP, and on Vipers to drain their Consume energy.

dark-templar.name: Dark Templar
dark-templar.shortDescription: Invisible assassin armed with a powerful warp scythe.
dark-templar.longDescription: The Dark Templar is permanently invisible. A single Dark Templar in a mineral line without detection can end the game.

shadow-stride.name: Shadow Stride
shadow-stride.description: Instantly teleports the Dark Templar a short distance.

archon-warp.name: Archon Warp
archon-warp.description: Two Templars merge to become a powerful Archon.

dark-templar.tacticalRole: Permanently cloaked assassin that can end games if the opponent lacks detection.
strengths: Always invisible — devastating without detection
High single-target damage per hit
Shadow Stride allows instant dashes for micro-intensive plays
weaknesses: Countered entirely by detection
Low health for the investment cost
Poor in large-scale frontal fights
dark-templar.usageTip: Drop Dark Templars in enemy worker lines when they're already engaged on another front. Cancel the timing if a detector is present.

archon.name: Archon
archon.shortDescription: Pure energy entity with almost impenetrable shields and area attack.
archon.longDescription: The Archon is born from the union of two templars. Its psionic power is so immense that it disintegrates nearby enemy units with each attack.
archon.tacticalRole: Massive energy entity with powerful shields and AoE attacks — deadly vs. biological units.
strengths: Massive health + shield pool makes it extremely durable
AoE attack deals bonus damage to biological units
Immune to neural parasite and mind control
weaknesses: Requires two Templar to create — sacrifices powerful spellcasters
Slow movement speed limits repositioning
No ranged attacks
archon.usageTip: Merge spent High Templars into Archons rather than letting them die with no energy. Pair Archons with Zealots for a fearsome ground ball.

observer.name: Observer
observer.shortDescription: Robotic scout unit invisible and permanent detector.
observer.longDescription: The Observer is the ultimate spy unit. It floats invisibly over the battlefield, revealing enemy hidden units.

surveillance-mode.name: Surveillance Mode
surveillance-mode.description: The Observer stops but increases its vision range by 25%.

observer.tacticalRole: Invisible permanent detector that provides crucial vision and detection across the battlefield.
strengths: Permanently cloaked — very hard to kill without detection
Provides sight of burrowed and cloaked units
Surveillance Mode doubles vision range
weaknesses: Fragile and slow without a speed upgrade
One Observer is often not enough vs. multi-prong threats
Revealed by enemy detectors and anti-cloak abilities
observer.usageTip: Always move an Observer with your main army and position others near enemy expansions. Scout for Widow Mines and Lurkers before advancing.

warp-prism.name: Warp Prism
warp-prism.shortDescription: Air transport capable of projecting an energy field for summons.
warp-prism.longDescription: The Warp Prism allows protoss to deploy armies anywhere on the map by becoming a mobile energy source.

phasing-mode.name: Phasing Mode
phasing-mode.description: Deploys to project an energy field that allows summoning Warp Gate units around it.

warp-prism.tacticalRole: Mobile pylon and transport that enables surprise attacks anywhere on the map.
strengths: Creates a warp-in zone anywhere on the map
Extremely fast in Phase Mode with speed upgrade
Can drop entire armies behind enemy lines
weaknesses: Fragile — dies quickly to focused fire
Expensive and high-priority target
Requires protection and constant micro
warp-prism.usageTip: Harass enemy mineral lines with Adepts or Immortals warped in near the Warp Prism. Always retreat the Prism before it dies.

immortal.name: Immortal
immortal.shortDescription: Heavy robotic unit with a barrier shield specialized against armored targets.
immortal.longDescription: The Immortal is designed to win duels against tanks and other heavy units. Its barrier automatically activates to absorb bursts of damage.

barrier.name: Barrier
barrier.description: Automatically activates a shield that absorbs up to 100 damage when the Immortal takes an attack.

immortal.tacticalRole: Heavy robotic frontliner with a barrier shield that hard-counters single-target burst damage.
strengths: Barrier absorbs up to 100 damage per activation — counters Marauders
Exceptional DPS against armored units
High base armor makes it extremely durable under fire
weaknesses: Slow and cannot retreat quickly
Barrier does not help vs. AoE splash
Weak against biological units (no bonus damage)
immortal.usageTip: Use Immortals as the frontline against heavy armored armies. Pair with Sentries to block path with Force Fields and protect from flanks.

colossus.name: Colossus
colossus.shortDescription: Massive robotic walker with thermal lances to sweep infantry armies.
colossus.longDescription: The Colossus towers over the battlefield, allowing it to climb cliffs. Its lasers sweep the ground, quickly eliminating groups of light units.

cliff-walk.name: Cliff Walk
cliff-walk.description: Allows the tall Colossus structure to walk up and down cliffs freely.

colossus.tacticalRole: Massive walker that sweeps through light bio armies with thermal lances, striding over cliffs.
strengths: Enormous AoE that tears through clustered light infantry
Can walk up and down cliffs freely
Effectively forces opponent to split or die to lasers
weaknesses: Massive tag — vulnerable to anti-air (Vikings, Corruptors)
Slow repositioning makes it a priority target
Expensive — hard to replace if lost
colossus.usageTip: Always send Vikings or use Stalkers to protect Colossi from anti-massive air attacks. Position them behind your ground army and let them sweep freely.

disruptor.name: Disruptor
disruptor.shortDescription: Robotic unit that launches highly unstable purification energy balls.
disruptor.longDescription: The Disruptor launches a Purification Nova that, after a brief delay, explodes dealing devastating damage. A single well-placed nova can wipe out an entire army.

purification-nova.name: Purification Nova
purification-nova.description: Launches a controllable energy ball that explodes after a few seconds, dealing massive area damage.

disruptor.tacticalRole: Volatile AoE devastator that deletes entire army compositions with well-aimed Purification Novas.
strengths: Single Nova can wipe out an entire bio ball
Forces opponent to immediately scatter and micro constantly
Low resource cost for the devastation potential
weaknesses: Long cooldown between shots
Nova is telegraphed — experienced players will dodge it
Very fragile if caught without army protection
disruptor.usageTip: Fire Novas at the edge of bio clumps (not the center) where players are slow to micro. Always keep the Disruptor behind your army.

phoenix.name: Phoenix
phoenix.shortDescription: Agile air fighter capable of firing while moving.
phoenix.longDescription: The Phoenix is the protoss air superiority unit. Its Graviton Beam can lift ground units, leaving them defenseless and out of combat.

graviton-beam.name: Graviton Beam
graviton-beam.description: Lifts an enemy ground unit into the air, preventing it from attacking or moving.

phoenix.tacticalRole: Lightning-fast fighter that harasses and lifts critical units with Graviton Beam.
strengths: Graviton Beam lifts high-value ground units out of combat
Fastest air unit in the game — extremely agile
Excellent at sniping Overlords to deny Zerg supply
weaknesses: Cannot attack ground units directly
Fragile against massed anti-air units
Graviton Beam requires manual targeting and casting
phoenix.usageTip: Open Phoenix to kill Overlords, deny Zerg supply, and lift Queens. Transition to adding Carriers or Void Rays after securing air control.

void-ray.name: Void Ray
void-ray.shortDescription: Attack ship with an energy beam that becomes more destructive against armored targets.
void-ray.longDescription: The Void Ray can focus its beam to melt structures and capital ships in seconds via its Prismatic Alignment.

prismatic-alignment.name: Prismatic Alignment
prismatic-alignment.description: Temporarily increases damage against armored units, but reduces movement speed.

void-ray.tacticalRole: Slow but powerful beam ship that melts armored units and structures with Prismatic Alignment.
strengths: Prismatic Alignment dramatically boosts damage vs. armored targets
Attacks both ground and air units
Durable for an air unit
weaknesses: Slow movement — easily kited by fast units
Prismatic Alignment reduces speed during activation
Countered hard by Vikings and Corruptors
void-ray.usageTip: Use Void Rays to overwhelm enemy structures during Prismatic Alignment. Activate it just before engaging to maximize burst output.

oracle.name: Oracle
oracle.shortDescription: Versatile support ship for scouting and surgical attacks.
oracle.longDescription: The Oracle is an invaluable utility unit. It can reveal invisible units, place stasis traps, or use its pulsar beam to annihilate workers.

revelation.name: Revelation
revelation.description: Reveals position and vision of enemy units in an area for an extended time.

stasis-ward.name: Stasis Ward
stasis-ward.description: Places an invisible trap that traps enemy units in stasis temporarily.

pulsar-beam.name: Pulsar Beam
pulsar-beam.description: Activates a high-power beam attack that consumes energy per second.

oracle.tacticalRole: Versatile support flyer that scouts, disables workers, traps units, and reveals the map.
strengths: Pulsar Beam destroys undefended workers in seconds
Revelation reveals all cloaked/burrowed units in a large area
Stasis Ward traps units for free in pre-placed ambushes
weaknesses: Fragile — easy to kill with basic anti-air
Pulsar Beam consumes energy rapidly
Requires constant micro to be efficient
oracle.usageTip: Open Oracle to scout the map and harass the opponent's economy. Pre-place Stasis Wards on ramps or expansion paths.

tempest.name: Tempest
tempest.shortDescription: Capital ship with the longest attack range of the protoss.
tempest.longDescription: The Tempest is designed to siege enemy positions from a safe distance. It is especially effective against other massive ships.
tempest.tacticalRole: Ultra-long-range capital ship designed to siege ground positions and demolish massive units.
strengths: Longest attack range of any Protoss unit
Bonus damage vs. massive units (Brood Lords, Ultralisks)
Forces opponent to reposition or lose units at max range
weaknesses: Very slow movement — hard to reposition quickly
Extremely expensive to mass
Cannot engage non-massive air units effectively
tempest.usageTip: Position Tempests just out of enemy range and fire from behind a Stalker/Zealot wall. Always protect from Vipers who can Abduct them.

carrier.name: Carrier
carrier.shortDescription: Massive air base that launches swarms of interceptors.
carrier.longDescription: The Carrier is the pride of the protoss fleet. It does not attack directly, but deploys robotic interceptors that overwhelm the enemy.

build-interceptor.name: Build Interceptor
build-interceptor.description: Automatically builds interceptors to replace those lost in combat.

carrier.tacticalRole: Protoss capital ship that overwhelms the battlefield with swarms of semi-autonomous Interceptors.
strengths: Builds free Interceptors that replace themselves during combat
High durability — shield + hull pool is massive
Deals significant sustained DPS vs. ground and air
weaknesses: Very slow and expensive to mass
Interceptors can be individually killed reducing DPS
Useless if caught by fast air units before Interceptors launch
carrier.usageTip: Group Carriers together for maximum Interceptor saturation. Always pair with Stalkers or Void Rays to ward off Viking and Corruptor threats.

mothership.name: Mothership
mothership.shortDescription: The ultimate flagship, capable of warping time and space.
mothership.longDescription: Only one Mothership can exist at a time. Its mere presence cloaks all nearby friendly army and can freeze time in critical zones.

cloak-field.name: Cloaking Field
cloak-field.description: Automatically cloaks all nearby friendly units and structures.

time-warp.name: Time Warp
time-warp.description: Creates a time bubble that drastically slows enemy unit movement and attack.

strategic-recall.name: Strategic Recall
strategic-recall.description: Teleports the Mothership and all nearby friendly units to a target Nexus.

mothership.tacticalRole: Unique flagship that cloaks all friendly units, warps armies with Recall, and freezes time.
strengths: Cloaking Field makes your entire army invisible
Strategic Recall can save your army from certain death
Time Warp stops enemy armies at critical chokepoints
weaknesses: Only one can exist at a time — losing it is catastrophic
Extremely expensive and slow to build
Priority target — opponent always focuses it first
mothership.usageTip: Use Recall to save a dying army or to drop troops at a new location. Use Time Warp at ramps to stall pushes while your forces regroup.
